title: Glowing Tunisian Panang-Paste Tagine
description: A silky, ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly dish featuring panang-paste, perfect for a sick day. This Tunisian-inspired recipe combines the aromatic flavors of cumin, coriander, and turmeric with the rich taste of Panang-paste.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onions and cook until they're translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the Panang-paste, cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and turmeric, and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth and coconut milk, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the tagine simmer for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the sliced almonds and chopped cilantro.
Transfer the tagine to a serving dish and flambe with a splash of rum or brandy, if desired.
Serve hot over rice or couscous, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
This dish is best served immediately, as the flavors and textures will hold best when fresh.
This recipe makes 4 servings and can be refrigerated or frozen for later use.
Note: if using a slow cooker, brown the onions and cook the spices in a skillet before transferring everything to the slow cooker and cooking on low for 2-3 hours.
Note: if using a Pressure cooker, brown the onions and cook the spices in a skillet before transferring everything to the pressure cooker and cooking on high for 10-15 minutes.
